# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a CloudFormation template for an IAM role that allows cross-account access for our CI/CD system. Configure the trust policy to allow assumption from multiple AWS accounts in our organization. Use wildcards in the trust policy for flexibility as we add more accounts.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: IAM Role for cross-account CI/CD access, allowing assumption from multiple accounts in an AWS Organization.

Parameters:
  AWSOrganizationID:
    Type: String
    Description: The ID of your AWS Organization (e.g., o-xxxxxxxxxx). This ensures only accounts within your organization can assume this role.
  CICDRoleName:
    Type: String
    Description: The name for the IAM Role.
    Default: CICDCrossAccountRole

Resources:
  CICDCrossAccountIAMRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      RoleName: !Ref CICDRoleName
      Description: Allows CI/CD systems to assume this role for cross-account operations within the organization.
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: '*' # This allows any AWS account to attempt assumption.
                       # The Condition below restricts this to only accounts within your specified organization.
            Action: sts:AssumeRole
            Condition:
              StringEquals:
                aws:PrincipalOrgID: !Ref AWSOrganizationID # This condition acts as the "wildcard" for accounts
                                                         # within your organization. Any account in the
                                                         # specified organization can assume this role.
      # ManagedPolicyArns:
      #   - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/ReadOnlyAccess # Example: Attach policies that define what this role CAN DO.
                                                 # Customize these based on the permissions your CI/CD needs.
                                                 # It's recommended to attach specific, least-privilege policies.
      # PermissionsBoundary: String # Optional: Use a permissions boundary for maximum permission allowed.

Outputs:
  CICDCrossAccountRoleARN:
    Description: The ARN of the IAM Role created for CI/CD cross-account access.
    Value: !GetAtt CICDCrossAccountIAMRole.Arn
